rule_metadata.author: NTT Security - Axel Olsson, Amata Anantaprayoon, Patrik Olson
rule_metadata.title: Phishing Proxy

rules.rule_text:
title: Phishing Proxy
id: b03d7953-62ab-48f6-b457-1a626850f17e
description: A phishing proxy typically relays the same requests/responses as the
  legitimate site, while also intercepting credentials and/or session cookies. This
  rule detects HTTP requests that are part of login flows on sites that are common
  in a corporate environment. It can also detect a subdomain pattern commonly used
  by EvilProxy.
status: test
author: NTT Security - Axel Olsson, Amata Anantaprayoon, Patrik Olson
date: '2023-12-09'
modified: '2024-08-16'
logsource:
  category: proxy
references:
- https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/12/07/star-blizzard-increases-sophistication-and-evasion-in-ongoing-attacks/
- https://github.com/kgretzky/evilginx2
- https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-341a
- https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/email-and-cloud-threats/cloud-account-takeover-campaign-leveraging-evilproxy-targets-top-level
- https://www.virustotal.com/gui/ip-address/154.29.75.192/relations
detection:
  selection_evilproxy_subdomain:
    cs-host|re: ^[a-f0-9]{8}-[a-f0-9]{8}\.
  selection_google:
    cs-method|cased: POST
    c-uri:
    - /signin/v2/identifier
    - /v3/signin/_/AccountsSignInUi/data/batchexecute
  selection_linkedin:
    cs-method|cased: POST
    c-uri|cased:
    - /uas/login-submit
    - /checkpooint/lg/login-submit
  selection_microsoft_live:
    cs-method|cased: POST
    c-uri:
    - /GetCredentialType.srf
    - /ppsecure/post.srf
    - /oauth20_authorize.srf
  selection_microsoft_online:
    cs-method|cased: POST
    c-uri:
    - /common/GetCredentialType
  selection_microsoft_adfs:
    cs-method|cased: POST
    c-uri|cased:
    - /adfs/ls
    c-uri-query|contains|cased:
    - email=
  filter:
  - cs-host|cased:
    - accounts.google.com
    - login.live.com
    - login.microsoftonline.com
    - login.microsoftonline.us
  - cs-host|endswith|cased:
    - .linkedin.com
    - .linkedin.cn
    - .mcas.ms
  condition: 1 of selection_* and not filter
falsepositives:
- Old domains are likely FP, check whois or similar data
- The user may not have entered any credentials. Look for suspicious logins or activity
  logs around this time
level: high
tags:
- attack.initial-access
